articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: 'Friend to All' - Zimbabwe Hails Foreign Policy Gains Since 2017
more.description: [263Chat] The government says its foreign policy of being a "friend to all and an enemy to none" has delivered major economic and diplomatic gains since 2017.

more.title: Ex-Health Registrar Wins Bail As He Appeals Abuse of Office Conviction
more.description: [263Chat] The High Court has granted US$500 bail to Collin Benyure, the former Registrar of the Medical and Dental Practitioners Council of Zimbabwe who was serving a 12-month jail term for criminal abuse of duty.

more.title: Zimbabwe nears signing of oil and gas agreement with Invictus for Cabora Bassa project
more.description: Zimbabwe is set to formalise its emerging oil and gas sector with the imminent signing of a Petroleum Production Sharing Agreement (PPSA), as Invictus Energy Ltd says the agreement is in its final stages and expected to be concluded this month. The post Zimbabwe nears signing of oil and gas agreement with Invictus for Cabora Bassa project appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Zimbabwe churches oppose Mnangagwa term extension bid after ‘prayerful’ reflection
more.description: The Zimbabwe Council of Churches (ZCC) has resolved to oppose the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment Bill No. 3 (2026) which seeks to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term of office following what it described as prayerful reflection and consultations. The post Zimbabwe churches oppose Mnangagwa term extension bid after ‘prayerful’ reflection appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Chapungu Comes Home: SA Hands Over Last Zimbabwe Bird After Over a Century
more.description: Chapungu Comes Home: SA Hands Over Last Zimbabwe Bird After Over a Century South Africa is set to return the last remaining Zimbabwe Soapstone Bird, known as Chapungu, along with ancestral human remains. The handover was officiated by South Africa’s Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture, Gayton McKenzie, marking a significant moment in efforts to [...] The post Chapungu Comes Home: SA Hands Over Last Zimbabwe Bird After Over a Century appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: Chamisa claims ‘fake news’ as activists accuse him of selling out
more.description: Former Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC) leader Nelson Chamisa has hit back at what he calls a “propaganda campaign of fake news” after being accused by some opposition activists of “selling out the struggle for democracy”. The accusations stem from Chamisa’s perceived lack of vigour in opposing the controversial Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3, which [...] The post Chamisa claims ‘fake news’ as activists accuse him of selling out appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: South Africa returns stolen human remains, sacred bird carving to Zimbabwe
more.description: AFP South Africa on Tuesday handed back to Zimbabwe ancestral human remains and a centuries-old stone carving of its sacred national emblem, the Zimbabwe bird, stolen more than 100 years ago during the colonial era. The restitution was part of a worldwide push for the repatriation of artefacts looted from African countries during colonisation. Eight [...] The post South Africa returns stolen human remains, sacred bird carving to Zimbabwe appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Troubled OK Zim closes Byo Lobengula St branch
more.description: Source: Troubled OK Zim closes Byo Lobengula St branch – herald Sikhulekelani Moyo, sikhu.moyo@chronicle.co.zw STRUGGLING retail giant OK Zimbabwe has closed its Lobengula branch in Bulawayo. This follows a plunge into “choppy waters” that has forced the 83-year-old retail chain to shut down several outlets across the country. Among the operations already affected are the Food [...] The post Troubled OK Zim closes Byo Lobengula St branch appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .
